A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly identifying 35 distinct businesses with 2018 reviews, the agent fetched only an arbitrary subset of business documents using a Mongo query with "limit": 1000 and then filtered client-side (function-call-3123561439902205723 + function-call-18060364738707330088), causing some target businesses to be missing and resulting in an undercount of 21.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

The agent correctly collected 2018-reviewed businesses and the business attributes, but its execute_python implementation (function-call-77011907421151122) only counted cases where BikeParking == 'True' (string) or any BusinessParking subfield evaluated to True, which undercounted and led to returning 21 instead of the correct 35.

After retrieving the 2018-reviewed businesses and the business attributes, the agent’s Python implementation applied overly strict parsing and truth checks on the attributes (e.g., only counting BikeParking when the value equaled the exact string 'True' and requiring at least one True flag in the BusinessParking dict), which led to undercounting and the final answer (21) not matching the ground truth (35).
